commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: feat(docker): add outbound proxy environment variable examples for Docker deployments
target.body: feat(docker): add outbound proxy environment variable examples for Docker deployments - Add commented HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY variables to docker-compose.yml - Document how to set the proxy port to match the host's actual proxy - Improve cross-platform proxy configuration guidance ## Summary - Problem: Users running OpenClaw in Docker behind a local proxy (e.g., Clash Verge/mihomo) need to manually configure proxy environment variables for outbound connectivity, but the default [docker-compose.yml](vscode-file://vscode-app/d:/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) does not provide clear guidance or examples. - Why it matters: Without explicit proxy configuration, outbound API requests (e.g., Telegram, Slack) may fail in restricted network environments, especially in regions where proxies are common. - What changed: Added commented example lines for HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY in [docker-compose.yml], with clear English instructions for replacing the proxy port and ensuring cross-platform compatibility. What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to application logic, CLI, or non-Docker deployment methods. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: - Missing detection / guardrail: - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Why this regressed now: - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Root cause: Lack of documented proxy configuration in Docker Compose led to user confusion and failed outbound connections in proxied environments. - Missing detection / guardrail: No prior example or documentation in [docker-compose.yml]. - Prior context: User reports and GitHub issues regarding proxy connectivity. - Why this regressed now: N/A (feature addition). - If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: N/A - Scenario the test should lock in: N/A - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: N/A - Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A - If no new test is added, why not: Documentation/config change only ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Users now have clear, documented examples for enabling outbound proxy support in Docker deployments by editing [docker-compose.yml]. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: [User runs OpenClaw in Docker] -> [No proxy config] -> [Outbound API requests may fail] After: [User runs OpenClaw in Docker] -> [Enables proxy config by uncommenting lines] -> [Outbound API requests succeed via proxy] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux, macOS, Windows (Docker Desktop) - Runtime/container: Docker Compose - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): Proxy running on host, port 7897 ### Steps 1. Uncomment and set the proxy environment variables in [docker-compose.yml] 2. Start the gateway container 3. Verify outbound API requests succeed (e.g., Telegram, Slack) ### Expected - Outbound requests are routed through the configured proxy and succeed. ### Actual - Outbound requests succeed when proxy is configured; fail otherwise in restricted environments. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) OpenClaw deployed in a container can access the Telegram API behind the GFW. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Outbound API requests from gateway container succeed via proxy on Linux and macOS. - Edge cases checked: Proxy port mismatch, missing proxy, and non-proxy environments. - What you did **not** verify: Non-Docker deployment methods. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Users may set incorrect proxy port or protocol. - Mitigation: Clear inline comments and documentation provided.
